name: Compound / Goal
text:
Capture the durable context before implementation.

Output:
- problem
- user
- success metric
- constraints
- non-goals

Everything that follows should optimize for this document.

name: Compound / Strategy
text:
Create or refresh the product-engineering strategy.

Output:
- diagnosis
- guiding principle
- target user
- success metric
- constraints
- work tracks
- near-term bet

Keep it short enough to guide future planning.

name: Compound / Ideate
text:
Explore possible directions before choosing a plan.

Generate 3-5 options. For each, include:
- user value
- implementation shape
- risk
- cost
- why it might win

Then recommend one path and explain the tradeoff.

name: Compound / Brainstorm
text:
Do not write code yet.

Ask only the questions needed to remove ambiguity. Then produce:
- requirements
- edge cases
- risks
- acceptance criteria

Stop there.

name: Compound / Plan
text:
Turn the requirements into an implementation plan.

Include:
- files to modify
- order of work
- risks
- tests
- rollback

Prefer the smallest reviewable implementation.

name: Compound / Work
text:
Execute the approved plan.

Work until complete. Continuously:
- verify assumptions
- run tests
- fix failures
- update progress

Only stop for missing product decisions or destructive actions.

name: Compound / Review
text:
Review from multiple viewpoints.

Look for:
- correctness
- simplicity
- security
- performance
- maintainability
- regression risk

Only report actionable findings.

name: Compound / Simplify
text:
Simplify the solution before compounding it.

Look for:
- avoidable complexity
- unclear naming
- too many concepts
- duplicated paths
- premature abstractions
- docs that no longer match behavior

Return a smaller version of the same idea.

name: Compound / Capture
text:
Capture reusable engineering knowledge.

Output:
Pattern:
Problem:
Solution:
Tradeoffs:
When to reuse:
When not to reuse:

Write so another engineer does not have to rediscover it.

name: Compound / Pulse
text:
Summarize the engineering cycle.

Output:
- completed
- learned
- outstanding risks
- technical debt
- highest-leverage next task

Recommend the next iteration.
